Subject: Re: Filtering outbound mail only.
Body: mouss wrote: > - for sendmail, this depends on how your situation. if sendmail is not > used to reinject filtered mail, you can just add a -o content_filter > under pickup in master.cf. If sendmail is used to reinject filtered > mail, this can't be used because you will have a loop. The solution is > to use multiple postfix instances If this is the consensus, then I think I'd rather move filtering based on destination address to the spam filter itself. I'd like to thank everyone for their input. mvg, Michiel

Subject: Re: [opensuse] Why do my messages get bounced
Body: Aaron Kulkis wrote: > Alexey Eremenko wrote: >> On Feb 7, 2008 1:11 PM, Dave Plater wrote: >>> For some reason or other my replies to list are being bounced, I've >>> opened a gmail account to send this message. >>> Please help >>> Dave Plater >>> -- >>> To unsubscribe, e-mail: assipqff+nmekprykass@opensuse.org >>> For additional commands, e-mail: qybuovjs+kcmh@opensuse.org >>> >>> >> >> Dave, on GMail you need to disable "rich text formatting" and use >> "simple text formatting". >> This is changeable after you click "reply" between the "editing" >> portion of GMail, and the "To/CC:" portion. > > Or just set it to use plain text as default. > > 95+% of messages are perfectly acceptable in plain text, > and not enhanced in the slightest by rich text or HTML. > > Thanks for your reply. The problem was yahoo's fault, I've solved it with a gmail account and a second nomail subscription with the opensuse list. I think it was due to an email I sent asking why a chinese IP bounced a spam report of mine saying that yahoo mail was blacklisted. Dave Plater

Subject: Re: [spambayes-dev] FW: [Spambayes] (no subject)
Body: Mark> It appears that a particular mail is malformed, and SpamBayes Mark> isn't robust enough. Mark> Looking inside the traceback, the code arond like 1371 of Mark> tokenize.py is: Mark> try: Mark> subjcharsetlist = email.Header.decode_header(x) Mark> except (binascii.Error, email.Errors.HeaderParseError): Mark> subjcharsetlist = [(x, 'invalid')] Mark> It looks like that except clause should be changed to include Mark> ValueError at least - eg: Mark> except (binascii.Error, email.Errors.HeaderParseError, Mark> ValueError): Mark> Could you please try that change and see if it goes away? I've Mark> also CCd spambayes-dev in the hope that someone there will offer Mark> some opinion if this is indeed a fix that should be checked in... I went ahead and checked it in. ;-) Skip _______________________________________________ spambayes-dev mailing list bsfegmjrr-hnq@python.org http://mail.python.org/mailman/listinfo/spambayes-dev

Body: 5 DIY Skills You Can Master Like Pro (Day 5): Smooth Drywall Learn how to fix damaged drywall before you paint. тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ тАМ Hi there Honey,Welcome to Day 5 of 5 Skills You Can Master to Feel like a DIY Pro. Every homeowner will run into a time where theyтАЩll need to patch, fill, replace, or repair drywall. The materials themselves are usually inexpensive, but labor and skill of getting a smooth finish is when the numbers start to add up. And don't fall into this common DIY trap: paint doesn't hide these mistakes тАФ it emphasizes them!But you can get all the answers here. What if you could DIY and save money by doing your own labor? After both installing new and repairing damaged drywall, I shared ALL of my secrets for how to get a professionally smooth-looking finish that's ready for paint in two blog posts. Here's the first! How To Fix Drywall PS. 5 out of 5 тАФ you did it! Thank you so much for letting me guide you through these series. If you learned something, would you mind replying to this email and letting me know? Or encouraging a friend to sign up for this series as well? My goal is to get all of us PERPETUAL PROJECT STARTERS to finally tackle their to-do lists and build their DIY confidence. Starting this next Tuesday, I've got a new weekly series for you: my most popular DIY projects to build your skills even more. In it, I'm sharing home repair, renovation, woodworking, and other tips. You don't have to do a thing; as part of completing this series, you're already enrolled for free. You've also got free access to my download libraries, so be on the lookout for the email with the subscribers-only password info. See you next week! 4671 Flint Hill RdAustell, GA 30096, USA Unsubscribe or Manage Preferences

Subject: Your Silver plan free trial has begun
Body: Welcome to your Flightradar24 Silver plan trial Silver YouтАЩre ready to start! Did you know you can use your account on any device? Simply download the app or visit the website and log in with your credentials. Let's start How to get the most out of your trial alert Set custom alerts You can create up to 10 custom alerts and filters for any flights you are interested in. Alerts can be sent via email or as push notifications. AR Launch AR view Using the app, you can point your camera at the sky and receive details for any planes flying overhead. flight Check flight status Search by flight number, airline, destination and origin to see schedule, estimated arrivals and any delays. With the Silver Plan, you have access to over 30 premium-only features. You can see a full breakdown of the features by plan here. gold Want even more features? Gold membership offers even more data including 365 days worth of aircraft and flight data, aeronautical charts and weather layers. See weather that aircraft are avoiding with cloud and precipitation layers. Gold subscribers have access to increased numbers of filters and alerts. See more details of the Gold Plan here. avtalk Our AvTalk podcast keeps you up to date with the fast pace of aviation bringing a combination of news, expert analysis, and engaging conversations with industry insiders. New episodes every Friday. Find out more here. avtalk Check out our tutorial videos here to get up to speed on how to use our main features. Check it out. If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact us. Best regards, The Flightradar24 team Track flights wherever you go apple store google store Connect with us twitter instagram linkedin facebook Unsubscribe Unsubscribe Preferences Kungsgatan 12 111 35 Stockholm app icon Open the app

Subject: Re: [Python-Dev] os.rename on windows
Body: On 4/30/07, Andrew Bennetts wrote: > Does MOVEFILE_REPLACE_EXISTING mean the rename over an existing file is actually > atomic? I cannot find any MSDN docs that say so (and I've seen some that > suggest to me that it probably isn't). Even though MSDN docs do not say it explicitly, I found some discussions claiming that MOVEFILE_REPLACE_EXISTING is atomic. However, after seeing your comment, I did a more thorough search and I too found some references claiming otherwise. As a last resort, I checked cygwin documentation which claims that it's rename() is POSIX.1 compliant. If I am not mistaken, POSIX.1 does require atomicity so I am curious how rename() is implemented there. I checked out the sources and I will try to find more about their implementation. I completely agree that without positive proof of atomicity, there is no point in making this code change. > Also, I assume this cannot replace files that are in use? A simple test shows that it can indeed replace files that are open. Thanks, Raghu _______________________________________________ Python-Dev mailing list zvllln-eum@python.org http://mail.python.org/mailman/listinfo/python-dev Unsubscribe: http://mail.python.org/mailman/options/python-dev/python-dev%40tangomu.com
